task_objective: Suppress Syncthing monitoring when no configured peers for the backup folder are connected, and immediately show the terminal LOCAL BACKUP SAVED - NO SYNCTHING PEERS ONLINE warning after a successful offline backup instead of the misleading SYNCTHING PREPARING delay.
design_decisions: Peer connectivity, not internet connectivity, gates monitoring: the matched folder's configured remote device IDs are intersected with connected devices from /rest/system/connections.
The local device ID from /rest/system/status is excluded during folder resolution so a folder shared with nobody is classified folder_not_shared and reuses the existing PATH NOT SHARED warning.
get_connection_totals() is preserved as a compatibility wrapper around the new structured ConnectionSnapshot parser.

The watcher re-evaluates relevant peer membership each poll tick and stops with a terminal no_connected_peers result when the last relevant peer disconnects; a failed connections poll keeps the last known peer set instead of stopping.

A shared frontend mapper (mapSyncthingFailureReason) translates backend reasons for both the lifecycle handoff and monitor poll failures, keeping not_configured and unexpected reasons silent while no_connected_peers maps to the new syncthing_no_peers status.
syncthing_no_peers renders with the existing amber warning style, fallback icon treatment, and terminal auto-hide; no failure toast is emitted and pre-game no-peer detection stays silent.
Only reason, phase, and configured/connected peer counts are logged; device IDs never leave the backend.
results: Backups without connected peers now skip watcher allocation and immediately publish the terminal no-peers warning; an active watch stops with the same terminal result if all relevant peers disconnect. Existing not_configured, api_unavailable, and path-not-shared behavior is unchanged.
validation: Passed tracked-file ruff check and format, ty check, 414 Python tests, 79 frontend tests, TypeScript type checking, Rollup build, and the pnpm supply-chain verification with no known vulnerabilities.
